comments: Configuration for VAMS v2.4 to v2.5 data migration (Full Table Copy V1 -> V2)
_comment_lookup: Asset storage table for building the assetId -> databaseId lookup cache
_comment_versions_tables: Asset Versions: V1 (source) and V2 (destination) table names
_comment_file_versions_tables: Asset File Versions: V1 (source) and V2 (destination) table names
_comment_metadata_versions_table: Asset File Metadata Versions: in-place backfill of databaseId:assetId field for new GSI
_comment_aws: AWS configuration (optional - override with CLI flags)
_comment_instructions: How to use this configuration file
_instructions: === SETUP ===
1. Get table names from CloudFormation stack outputs:
aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name your-vams-stack \
--query 'Stacks[0].Outputs[?contains(OutputKey, `Table`)].{Key:OutputKey,Value:OutputValue}' \
--output table
2. Update configuration with actual resource names:
- asset_storage_table_name: AssetStorageTable (PK=databaseId, SK=assetId) - used for lookup cache
- asset_versions_storage_table_name_v1: Old V1 table (PK=assetId, SK=assetVersionId)
- asset_versions_storage_table_name_v2: New V2 table (PK=databaseId:assetId, SK=assetVersionId)
- asset_file_versions_storage_table_name_v1: Old V1 table (PK=assetId:assetVersionId, SK=fileKey)
- asset_file_versions_storage_table_name_v2: New V2 table (PK=databaseId:assetId:assetVersionId, SK=fileKey)
- asset_file_metadata_versions_storage_table_name: Metadata versions table (PK=databaseId:assetId:assetVersionId) - in-place backfill
=== EXECUTION ===
3. Run dry-run first (HIGHLY RECOMMENDED):
python v2.4_to_v2.5_migration.py --config v2.4_to_v2.5_migration_config.json --dry-run
4. Test with limited items (optional):
python v2.4_to_v2.5_migration.py --config v2.4_to_v2.5_migration_config.json --limit 10 --dry-run
5. Run production migration:
python v2.4_to_v2.5_migration.py --config v2.4_to_v2.5_migration_config.json
=== MIGRATION DETAILS ===
Phase 1: Build lookup cache - scans asset storage table to map assetId -> databaseId
Phase 2: Migrate asset versions - full copy from V1 to V2 with new key schema (PK = databaseId:assetId)
Phase 3: Migrate file versions - full copy from V1 to V2 with new key schema (PK = databaseId:assetId:assetVersionId)
Phase 4: Backfill metadata versions - add databaseId:assetId field to AssetFileMetadataVersionsStorageTable for new GSI
Phase 5: Verify - count records in V1 and V2 tables, verify V2 counts, key structure, and metadata backfill
=== KEY SCHEMA CHANGES ===
Asset Versions V1: PK = assetId, SK = assetVersionId
Asset Versions V2: PK = databaseId:assetId, SK = assetVersionId
File Versions V1: PK = assetId:assetVersionId, SK = fileKey
File Versions V2: PK = databaseId:assetId:assetVersionId, SK = fileKey (+ databaseId:assetId GSI field)
=== SAFETY FEATURES ===
- dry_run: Test without making changes (default: false)
- limit: Test with subset of items (default: null = all items)
- batch_size: DynamoDB batch write size (default: 25, max: 25)
- Idempotent: Safe to re-run - PutItem overwrites existing V2 records
- Continue-on-error: Individual batch failures don't stop the migration
=== NOTES ===
- Command-line arguments override config file settings
- Migration is read-only in dry-run mode
- No V1 data is deleted - this is a copy operation to new V2 tables
- V1 tables remain intact and can be used as a rollback source
